member.name: Max Wilkinson
member.synopsis: Max Wilkinson is the Liberal Democrat MP for Cheltenham , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. He currently undertakes the role of Liberal Democrat Spokesperson (Home Affairs).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Primary Education: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps her Department is taking to support single-form entry primary schools with reduced student numbers with funding levels.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government recognises the essential role that small schools play in their communities.All schools have benefited from the increase to core funding allocated through the schools national funding formula (NFF) in the 2026/27 financial year, including th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Migrant Workers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, pursuant to the Answer of 1 July 2026 to Question 13580, when her Department will publish the economic and equality impact assessments for the earned settlement system.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The consultation for the earned settlement model, as proposed in ‘A Fairer Pathway to Settlement’, was open to the public between 20 November 2025 and 12 February 2026.Once the final model has been decided by the Home Secretary, the economic and equality ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Shabir Ahmed
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what the cost to the public purse was of steps taken by her Department to have Shabir Ahmed deported.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Rochdale child sexual exploitation group preyed on the young and vulnerable and their appalling crimes have no place in our society; our thoughts are first and foremost with the victims of these appalling crimes.For his leading role in these crimes, S...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, if her Department will publish the list of infrastructure projects that will not proceed in order to fund the Defence Investment Plan.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Departments have been asked to contribute 1p in every £1 of their capital budgets to fund the Defence Investment Plan. Departments will decide how to deliver the required savings, prioritising cancellations, delays and efficiencies in lower‑value programm...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: VAT

writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to improving the quality and sustainability of our housing stock, through improvements such as low carbon heating, insulation, solar panels, and batteries. Installations of qualifying energy-saving materials (ESMs) in resident...

writtenQuestions.answerText: Whilst calculation and decision-making processes are set out in legislation, and decision makers must act in accordance with those statutory provisions, the Child Maintenance Service (CMS) is committed to ensuring victims and survivors of abuse get the he...

writtenQuestions.heading: Prescription Drugs: Shortages
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of the amount of time spent by pharmacists trying to source medications during shortages.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No assessment of the amount of time spent by pharmacists trying to source medications during shortages has been made. However, this issue has been considered when we consulted on proposals to enable pharmacist flexibilities when dispensing medicines. More...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Prescription Drugs: Shortages
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to reduce the time spent by pharmacists trying to source medications during shortages.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No assessment of the amount of time spent by pharmacists trying to source medications during shortages has been made. However, this issue has been considered when we consulted on proposals to enable pharmacist flexibilities when dispensing medicines. More...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Pharmacy: Business Rates
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of between increased business rates on the level of closures of community pharmacies.

writtenQuestions.heading: Pharmacy: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps is the Government taking to ensure that pharmacies are sustainably funded for the increasing costs and staff time involved in sourcing medicines during supply shortages.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has recently increased funding for the community pharmacy sector by £340 million in 2026/27, a 10% cash uplift that supports pharmacies across England and patients' access to medicines. This builds on the 19% uplift to the community pharmac...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
